data.title: Is Fnac Darty (ENXTPA:FNAC) Turning Product Passports Into a Lasting Circular-Economy Advantage?
data.description:
• Fnac Darty has expanded its Product Passport to all large household appliances, using unique QR codes to give customers access to life-cycle and repair information, with a goal of equipping more than 1 million products by the end of 2026.
  • This broad rollout underlines how the group is using traceability and repairability tools to deepen its circular-economy positioning and sustainability credentials in Europe.
  • We will now examine how this expanded Product Passport program shapes Fnac Darty’s investment narrative, particularly around traceability and repairability leadership.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 109 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is Fnac Darty's Investment Narrative?

To own Fnac Darty, you need to believe the group can turn modest revenue growth and thin margins into more resilient profitability, while managing a relatively new management team and a balance sheet where interest costs still bite. The expanded Product Passport looks more like a medium-term brand and regulatory asset than a near-term earnings catalyst, so it probably does not change the immediate focus on upcoming 2025–2026 results, debt servicing, and integration of projects like the Unieuro financing. That said, it reinforces the company’s pitch as a European leader in repairability and circular retail, which could influence customer loyalty and policy relationships over time. Given the recent share price drift higher and still-elevated P/E, execution risk around these initiatives remains front and center.

Fnac Darty's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Simply Wall St Community members see fair values between €36.83 and a very large €162.88, highlighting sharply different expectations. Set that against Fnac Darty’s thin profit margins and interest coverage issues, and you can see why it pays to weigh several viewpoints on where the business might head next.

See our latest analysis for Cactus.

At a share price of US$53.47, Cactus has seen strong near term momentum, with a 30 day share price return of 16.21% and a 90 day gain of 34.18%. data.title: Did Assured’s Move Into Annuity Reinsurance Just Shift Assured Guaranty's (AGO) Investment Narrative?
data.description:
• Earlier in 2026, Assured Guaranty Ltd. acquired Bermuda-based life and annuity reinsurer Warwick Re Limited, renaming it Assured Life Reinsurance Ltd. and positioning the business to reinsure fixed-term and pension risk transfer annuities.
  • This move gives Assured Guaranty an immediate operating platform in the annuity reinsurance space, adding a new line of risk exposure and revenue diversification beyond its traditional financial guaranty operations.
  • We’ll now examine how Assured Guaranty’s entry into annuity reinsurance through Assured Life Reinsurance could reshape the company’s investment narrative.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 109 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is Assured Guaranty's Investment Narrative?

For Assured Guaranty, the big-picture thesis still rests on a specialist insurer trading at what many see as a discount to both analyst and community fair values, with high reported earnings quality, solid board independence and a long-tenured management team. In the short term, investors are watching how earnings hold up as consensus points to profit and revenue declines over the next few years and return on equity that remains modest. The stock’s weak 1‑year total return and recent pullback suggest expectations are already subdued. The Warwick Re deal, now Assured Life Reinsurance, adds a new annuity reinsurance platform, which could gradually shift the narrative from a pure financial guaranty story toward a more diversified risk and revenue mix, but its financial impact is unlikely to be immediate or to override existing earnings and ROE concerns.

However, investors should be aware of the risks around forecast earnings declines and capital allocation. Despite retreating, Assured Guaranty's shares might still be trading above their fair value and there could be some more downside. Discover how much.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s single fair value estimate clusters around US$180.58 per share, implying very large upside from current levels. Set against concerns over forecast earnings declines and low projected return on equity, this gap underlines how differently market participants can view Assured Guaranty’s prospects and why it can pay to compare several viewpoints before forming a conclusion.

data.title: Returns On Capital Are A Standout For Arrow Exploration (CVE:AXL)
data.description:
If you're not sure where to start when looking for the next multi-bagger, there are a few key trends you should keep an eye out for. Amongst other things, we'll want to see two things; firstly, a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and secondly, an expansion in the company's amount of capital employed. Put simply, these types of businesses are compounding machines, meaning they are continually reinvesting their earnings at ever-higher rates of return. Speaking of which, we noticed some great changes in Arrow Exploration's (CVE:AXL) returns on capital, so let's have a look.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Is Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)?

Just to clarify if you're unsure, ROCE is a metric for evaluating how much pre-tax income (in percentage terms) a company earns on the capital invested in its business. Analysts use this formula to calculate it for Arrow Exploration:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.26 = US$20m ÷ (US$94m - US$17m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Therefore, Arrow Exploration has an ROCE of 26%. That's a fantastic return and not only that, it outpaces the average of 8.4% earned by companies in a similar industry.

See our latest analysis for Arrow Exploration

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Arrow Exploration compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering Arrow Exploration for free.

What The Trend Of ROCE Can Tell Us

We're delighted to see that Arrow Exploration is reaping rewards from its investments and is now generating some pre-tax profits. About five years ago the company was generating losses but things have turned around because it's now earning 26% on its capital. And unsurprisingly, like most companies trying to break into the black, Arrow Exploration is utilizing 151% more capital than it was five years ago. We like this trend, because it tells us the company has profitable reinvestment opportunities available to it, and if it continues going forward that can lead to a multi-bagger performance.

In another part of our analysis, we noticed that the company's ratio of current liabilities to total assets decreased to 18%, which broadly means the business is relying less on its suppliers or short-term creditors to fund its operations. Therefore we can rest assured that the growth in ROCE is a result of the business' fundamental improvements, rather than a cooking class featuring this company's books.

Our Take On Arrow Exploration's ROCE

In summary, it's great to see that Arrow Exploration has managed to break into profitability and is continuing to reinvest in its business. And with the stock having performed exceptionally well over the last five years, these patterns are being accounted for by investors. With that being said, we still think the promising fundamentals mean the company deserves some further due diligence.

Arrow Exploration does have some risks, we noticed 2 warning signs (and 1 which can't be ignored) we think you should know about.

Arrow Exploration is not the only stock earning high returns. If you'd like to see more, check out our free list of companies earning high returns on equity with solid fundamentals.

data.title: Why New Fortress Energy (NFE) Is Up 22.5% After Brazil LNG Deal And New Partnerships
data.description:
• In recent days, New Fortress Energy has gained attention after outlining new partnerships, a South American expansion via a Brazilian deal, and progress on its LNG technology, while still contending with operational losses, high debt, and regulatory hurdles.
  • An interesting angle for investors is how these growth-focused partnerships and technology advances are emerging alongside persistent questions about the company’s long-term financial resilience.
  • We’ll now examine how the South American expansion via the Brazilian partnership shapes New Fortress Energy’s broader investment narrative and risk profile.

Advertisement

What Is New Fortress Energy's Investment Narrative?

To own New Fortress Energy today, you really have to believe that its LNG platform, technology and regional footholds can eventually outweigh a history of large losses, heavy leverage and difficult financing. The recent Brazilian partnership and South American expansion story sit right at the center of that belief: they support the idea of future scale and relevance, but arrive just months after forbearance deals on key loans and concerns about liquidity and potential default. The sharp share price jump around the news suggests the market sees it as at least directionally positive, yet the core short term catalysts still look tied to debt restructuring progress, covenant compliance and any evidence that new contracts translate into more stable cash flows. Until those improve, bankruptcy and dilution risk remain front of mind, even with growth headlines.

However, those same growth moves sit alongside real questions about balance sheet pressure that investors should understand. The analysis detailed in our New Fortress Energy valuation report hints at an deflated share price compared to its estimated value.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Six fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community stretch from below US$0.10 to above US$8, showing just how far apart private investors are on New Fortress Energy. Set that against the recent price surge on Brazilian expansion news and ongoing debt forbearance, and it becomes clear you are weighing very different readings of the same balance sheet and growth story.

data.title: Is Swiss Re’s (SWX:SREN) New Life Health Lead Hinting at a Sharper Income-Focused Strategy?
data.description:
• Swiss Re has promoted Christophe Heck to market head Life Health for France, BeNeLux and Switzerland, based in Zurich and leading the group’s regional life and health reinsurance team.
  • While this leadership move refines Swiss Re’s regional life and health focus, current investor attention appears more closely tied to the stock’s income appeal and upcoming earnings catalysts.
  • Next, we’ll examine how Swiss Re’s income appeal and upcoming earnings update could influence the company’s broader investment narrative.

Advertisement

What Is Swiss Re's Investment Narrative?

To own Swiss Re, you really need to be comfortable with a classic reinsurance trade-off: attractive income and apparently undemanding valuation on one side, and inherently lumpy earnings and catastrophe exposure on the other. The recent promotion of Christophe Heck to lead Life & Health across France, BeNeLux and Switzerland looks more like fine tuning than a catalyst that would move the stock in the near term. Short term, the market still seems focused on the upcoming earnings release, any surprises in reserve development, and how management frames catastrophe risk and capital returns after a weak share price spell. The leadership change could help execution in Life & Health over time, but it does not materially change the near term risk that a bad loss year or adverse reserving could pressure results and sentiment again.

But one key risk could quickly change how investors see that income profile. Despite retreating, Swiss Re's shares might still be trading above their fair value and there could be some more downside. Discover how much.

Those record fourth quarter numbers and the FineMark deal come after a mixed stretch for investors, with a 90 day share price return of 5.26% and a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 13.78%, suggesting short term momentum has picked up while longer term returns have been much softer.

If this kind of bank earnings story has your attention, it could be a good moment to widen your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With shares down on a 1 year view but trading at a reported 47% discount to one intrinsic value estimate and below the average analyst target, you have to ask: is there a genuine opportunity here, or is the market already factoring in future growth?

Advertisement

Preferred P/E of 13.8x: Is it justified?

Commerce Bancshares currently trades on a P/E of 13.8x, which prices the stock above the wider US banks industry average of 11.8x but roughly in line with the 14x peer group average.

The P/E ratio links the current share price to earnings. A higher multiple usually means the market is asking you to pay more today for each dollar of profit. For a bank like CBSH, that often reflects views on the quality and durability of earnings rather than rapid growth expectations.

Here, the picture is mixed. On one hand, earnings have grown 3.6% per year over the past 5 years, with a stronger 8.6% earnings growth figure over the last year and current net profit margins of 33.1% compared to 32% a year ago. On the other hand, CBSH's Return on Equity is 15%, which is classed as low against a 20% benchmark. In addition, earnings are forecast to grow 6.7% per year, slower than both the broader US market and the US banks industry.

Compared with the US banks industry average P/E of 11.8x and an estimated fair P/E of 12.4x, CBSH's 13.8x multiple sits at a premium level. The market could potentially move toward aligning with that fair ratio over time. This suggests investors are currently paying more than both the industry and the modelled fair level for each dollar of earnings, despite growth forecasts that are not especially high.

data.title: Novavax (NVAX) Is Up 14.5% After Licensing Matrix-M Adjuvant Platform To Pfizer – Has The Bull Case Changed?
data.description:
• In January 2026, Novavax announced a non-exclusive worldwide License and Option Agreement granting Pfizer access to its Matrix-M adjuvant technology for vaccine development in up to two infectious disease areas, in return for a US$30,000,000 upfront payment and the potential for additional milestones and royalties.
  • The deal effectively shifts development and commercialization responsibilities to Pfizer while positioning Novavax as a specialist supplier of its proprietary adjuvant, potentially broadening Matrix-M’s use across Pfizer’s vaccine portfolio.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this Matrix-M licensing partnership with Pfizer may influence Novavax’s investment narrative and future revenue visibility.

This technology could replace computers: discover 23 stocks that are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Is Novavax's Investment Narrative?

For anyone considering Novavax, the core belief now is that this is evolving into more of a vaccine-technology and royalty platform than a fully integrated vaccine marketer. The new Matrix-M licensing deal with Pfizer fits squarely into that shift: Novavax gets a US$30,000,000 cash injection and exposure to up to two Pfizer vaccine programs, while avoiding the heavy spend and execution risk of late-stage trials and commercialization. In the short term, key catalysts remain execution of the Sanofi collaboration on Nuvaxovid, clarity on any further Matrix-M partnerships, and how quickly the Pfizer agreement converts into concrete supply deals. The main risks do not disappear, but they change shape: dependence on partners, negative equity and debt coverage pressures, plus activist calls for a sale or broader strategic overhaul all remain central to the story.

However, one risk that could matter more than it first appears is partner execution and control. Novavax's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Nine Simply Wall St Community fair values for Novavax stretch from US$6.64 to US$100, reflecting very different views on the company. Against that backdrop, the Matrix-M deal with Pfizer sharpens the focus on execution risk, partner dependence and how much of Novavax’s future value might sit in licensing rather than its own product sales.

data.title: BayCom Corp (NASDAQ:BCML) Annual Results Just Came Out: Here's What Analysts Are Forecasting For This Year
data.description:
Last week saw the newest annual earnings release from BayCom Corp (NASDAQ:BCML), an important milestone in the company's journey to build a stronger business. It was a credible result overall, with revenues of US$96m and statutory earnings per share of US$2.18 both in line with analyst estimates, showing that BayCom is executing in line with expectations. This is an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance in its report, look at what experts are forecasting for next year, and see if there has been any change to expectations for the business. Readers will be glad to know we've aggregated the latest statutory forecasts to see whether the analysts have changed their mind on BayCom after the latest results.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Taking into account the latest results, the most recent consensus for BayCom from three analysts is for revenues of US$105.4m in 2026. If met, it would imply a solid 9.3% increase on its revenue over the past 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are predicted to step up 17% to US$2.58. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of US$103.1m and earnings per share (EPS) of US$2.57 in 2026. There doesn't appear to have been a major change in sentiment following the results, other than the small lift in revenue estimates.

Check out our latest analysis for BayCom

Even though revenue forecasts increased, there was no change to the consensus price target of US$31.67, suggesting the analysts are focused on earnings as the driver of value creation. There's another way to think about price targets though, and that's to look at the range of price targets put forward by analysts, because a wide range of estimates could suggest a diverse view on possible outcomes for the business. The most optimistic BayCom analyst has a price target of US$32.00 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at US$31.00. This is a very narrow spread of estimates, implying either that BayCom is an easy company to value, or - more likely - the analysts are relying heavily on some key assumptions.

Taking a look at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can understand these forecasts is to see how they compare to both past performance and industry growth estimates. It's clear from the latest estimates that BayCom's rate of growth is expected to accelerate meaningfully, with the forecast 9.3% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2026 noticeably faster than its historical growth of 4.6% p.a. over the past five years. Other similar companies in the industry (with analyst coverage) are also forecast to grow their revenue at 9.0% per year. BayCom is expected to grow at about the same rate as its industry, so it's not clear that we can draw any conclusions from its growth relative to competitors.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most obvious conclusion is that there's been no major change in the business' prospects in recent times, with the analysts holding their earnings forecasts steady, in line with previous estimates. There was also an upgrade to revenue estimates, although as we saw earlier, forecast growth is only expected to be about the same as the wider industry. The consensus price target held steady at US$31.67, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.

With that in mind, we wouldn't be too quick to come to a conclusion on BayCom. Long-term earnings power is much more important than next year's profits. We have estimates - from multiple BayCom analysts - going out to 2027, and you can see them free on our platform here.

Another thing to consider is whether management and directors have been buying or selling stock recently. We provide an overview of all open market stock trades for the last twelve months on our platform, here.

data.title: A Look At CBRE Group’s (CBRE) Valuation As AI Data Center Mandate Signals Deeper Digital Infrastructure Push
data.description:
CBRE Group (CBRE) is back on investors’ radar after being engaged by Bitzero Holdings as real estate broker for AI focused Nordic data center sites, emphasizing its push into digital infrastructure services.

See our latest analysis for CBRE Group.

While the share price has eased slightly in the last week, CBRE’s 30 day share price return of 2.74% and year to date share price return of 5.64% sit against a stronger backdrop, with total shareholder returns of 19.91% over one year and roughly 2x over three years. This suggests recent news around AI data centers and senior hires is feeding into a longer running improvement in how investors view the company’s growth and risk profile.

If CBRE’s push into AI linked real assets catches your attention, it could be worth broadening your search across high growth tech and AI stocks as you look for other potential ideas in this theme.

With CBRE trading around $169.23 and sitting roughly 9.5% below the average analyst price target of $185.33, investors may question whether there is still upside to capture here or if markets are already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 8.4% Undervalued

CBRE Group's most followed narrative puts fair value around $184.83, which sits above the last close of $169.23 and frames the recent AI data center news inside a longer running thesis about growth, margins and capital allocation.

The strong balance sheet and improved cash flow position allow CBRE to invest aggressively in M&A and principal investments, potentially driving higher future earnings and improved financial performance during economic downturns. CBRE's successful integration of Turner & Townsend is projected to enhance the project management segment's growth and margin profile over time, providing increased SOP margins and contributing to earnings growth through operational synergies and efficiency gains.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what powers that higher fair value? The most followed narrative leans on compounded revenue growth, thicker margins, and a richer earnings multiple tied to those cash flow assumptions.

Result: Fair Value of $184.83 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, there are still clear watchpoints, including tariff related uncertainty that could hit transactional revenue, and interest rate swings that could disrupt capital raising and large project timelines.

Find out about the key risks to this CBRE Group narrative.

Another View: Current P/E Paints A Richer Picture

That 8.4% undervalued fair value sits alongside a P/E of 40.4x, which is higher than the US Real Estate industry at 29.5x, the peer average at 28.8x, and our fair ratio of 25.8x. In practice, that gap means less room for error if growth or margins fall short, so which signal do you trust more?

data.title: A Look At Planet Fitness (PLNT) Valuation After Strong January Membership Trends And Supportive Analyst Ratings
data.description:
Recent analyst commentary around Planet Fitness (PLNT) has focused on strong January membership trends, with surveys pointing to both legacy and new Black Card perks helping drive sign ups during this key period.

See our latest analysis for Planet Fitness.

Even with positive headlines around January sign ups and new Black Card perks, Planet Fitness shares have recently been under pressure, with a 30 day share price return of 13.39% decline and a year to date share price return of 13.91% decline, while the 5 year total shareholder return of 31.21% shows a very different longer term picture.

If Planet Fitness’s recent moves have caught your eye, this can be a good moment to broaden your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With PLNT shares down over the past year but analysts setting price targets well above the recent US$94.47 close, you have to ask: is Planet Fitness quietly undervalued, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 27.6% Undervalued

With Planet Fitness shares last closing at $94.47 and the most followed narrative pointing to a fair value of $130.41, the gap between price and story is hard to ignore.

Rising Black Card penetration, combined with proven ability to implement price increases with minimal impact on churn, signals future near-term potential for further price hikes at this higher margin tier, directly benefitting average revenue per member and overall earnings.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what kind of revenue climb and margin profile has to hold up for that valuation to stack up? The core assumptions lean heavily on membership quality, not just membership count.

Result: Fair Value of $130.41 (UNDERVALUED)

However, it is worth keeping an eye on higher member attrition from click to cancel, as well as the risk that faster club expansion pressures franchisee economics.

Another View: High Multiple, Different Message

That 27.6% gap to the US$130.41 fair value hinges on long term growth assumptions, but the current P/E of 38.1x tells a different story. It sits well above both peers at 17.2x and the fair ratio of 23.6x. This points to meaningful valuation risk if expectations cool.
